title: Protocol for automated multivariate quantitative-image-based cytometry analysis by fluorescence microscopy of asynchronous adherent cells
author: Besse, Laetitia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
In this page, we presented information about the image analysis process that we used in the work: 

  "Protocol for automated multivariate quantitative-image-based cytometry analysis by fluorescence microscopy of asynchronous adherent cells "   

 Authors:  Laetitia Besse  1,6,7 , Typhaine Rumiac  2,3,4,    5,6   ,   Anne Reynaud-Angelin  2,3,4 , Cédric Messaoudi  1 , Marie-Noëlle Soler  1 , Sarah A E Lambert  2,3,4,8,* , Vincent Pennaneach  2,3,4,7,8,*   

   

  1  Institut Curie, Université PSL, CNRS UAR2016, Inserm US43, Université Paris-Saclay, Multimodal Imaging Center, 91400 Orsay, France. 

  2  Institut Curie, Université PSL, CNRS UMR3348, Orsay, France. 

  3  Université Paris-Saclay, CNRS UMR3348, Orsay, France. 

  4  Equipe Labélisée Ligue Nationale Contre Le Cancer, 91400 Orsay, France. 

  5  Inovarion, 75005 Paris, France. 

  6  These authors contributed equally to the work. 

  7  Technical contacts:  Laetitia.Besse@curie.fr  and  Vincent.Pennaneach@curie.fr  

  8  Lead contacts:  Sarah.Lambert@curie.fr  and  Vincent.Pennaneach@curie.fr  

 * Correspondence authors:  Sarah.Lambert@curie.fr  and  Vincent.Pennaneach@curie.fr  

   

 Here, we present a protocol for multivariate quantitative image-based cytometry (QIBC) analysis by fluorescence microscopy of asynchronous adherent cells. We describe steps for the preparation, treatment, and fixation of cells, sample staining, and imaging for QIBC. We then detail image analysis with our open source Fiji-script developped for QIBC, and present multiparametric data visualization. Our QIBC Fiji-script integrates modern artificial intelligence-based tools, applying deep-learning, for robust automated nuclei segmentation with minimal user adjustments, a major asset for efficient QIBC analysis. For complete details on the use and execution of this protocol, please refer to Besse et al. (2023).

keywords: Fiji, Nuclei segmentation, Fiji's macro, StarDist, QIBC, cell cycle, SpotFire
